key_insight: SpikeDS uses dual sparsity in a 3D spiking transformer to detect subtle perineural invasion signals in MRI more efficiently.
clinical_relevance: This could improve preoperative risk stratification for cholangiocarcinoma by helping identify patients at higher risk of poor prognosis.
method: Binary spike communication reduces activation cost.
Firing-rate-based window pruning focuses attention on salient 3D regions.
Cross-window attention preserves global context even when some windows are pruned.
evidence_signal: In a 139-patient cohort with 5-fold cross-validation, SpikeDS reached an AUC of 0.753 while using 14.4 mJ, outperforming the best baseline in both accuracy and energy efficiency.
watch_item: The results come from a single clinical cohort, so broader validation and deployment testing are still needed before routine use.
paper_title: SpikeDS: Dual Sparsity Spikformer for Perineural Invasion Prediction in 3D MRI
